findings.description: run_with_retries resets attempt=0 each iteration before incrementing, so the loop condition (1 < MAX_RETRIES) is permanently true and a consistently-failing job spins forever at 2s/iteration, never returning and never counting as failed.

poll_loop is async but calls blocking time.sleep instead of await asyncio.sleep, freezing the entire event loop (and every other coroutine in the web service) for a full second on every empty-queue poll.

poll_loop awaits nothing and runs run_with_retries/execute_job (including the blocking subprocess.run on line 52) directly on the event loop thread, blocking all other async work for the full duration of each job instead of offloading to an executor.
JobQueue.pop uses list.pop(0), which is O(n) due to element shifting, making a drain of N jobs O(n^2); a collections.deque with popleft() would be O(1).
Because of the attempt reset on line 69, the backoff term (attempt + 1) is always 1, so the intended escalating backoff degrades to a flat 2s and never grows.
